findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:113; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:169; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:259; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:127; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:187; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:299. The first three POV blocks each culminate in a clarified private thesis and then a departure beat, so the chapter keeps replaying the same structural move instead of compounding consequence.
findings.problem: The chapter's parallel-aftermath design is controlled, but it does not generate enough distinct outward consequence to clear award-level standards. Jet, Spike, and Faye all arrive at a sharpened private recognition and then exit on essentially the same return motion, which leaves the chapter feeling suspended rather than cumulative.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate one of the middle scene turns so the chapter's end-state changes on the page. The cleanest targets are snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:113, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:169, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:259, plus the exit beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:127, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:187, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:299. Keep the solitude, but let one scene produce a concrete external consequence or decision that the next scene inherits instead of another quiet walk-back.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least one of the scene endings anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:127, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:187, or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:299 now includes a concrete outward consequence or decision that alters the chapter's final situation; FAIL if all three scenes still end as private recognition plus departure with no new cost, contact, or obligation.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:149; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:157; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:169; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:179. Spike's section shifts from concrete recollection into polished moral thesis statements such as the abstracted 'moral revelation' sentence and the eyesight metaphor.
findings.problem: Spike's interior narration drifts away from the style-bible brief for his private register. Instead of sparse, concrete, flat self-scrutiny, the passage gives him finished moral commentary that sounds authorially composed rather than like Spike thinking under pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast the Spike passage around the memory objects already on the page: crates, route work, the manifest note, Julia's image, smoke, and the rooftop at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:149, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:157, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:169, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:179. Let the self-indictment emerge from concrete recall and short flat admissions instead of maxims.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised span anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:149 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:177 keeps Spike's guilt legible but delivers it mostly through concrete remembered details and short flat statements, with no sentence functioning as a standalone moral maxim; FAIL if the passage still reads like Spike composing a retrospective essay about what kind of man he was.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:359. Ed says, 'That was a long time ago, huh,' which reproduces a calibration-only style-bible example line nearly verbatim.
findings.problem: The chapter copies a calibration-only example line into live dialogue. The style bible explicitly reserves example lines for register anchoring rather than reproduction, so this reads as template leakage instead of earned speech.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace Ed's spoken beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:359 and, if needed, retune the setup/payoff at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:351 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:363 so the emotional drop stays brief, plain, and pressure-marked without echoing the style-bible sample.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the spoken beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:359 is no longer verbatim or near-verbatim to the style-bible example while still signaling Ed's shift into short, grammatically standard speech; FAIL if the line remains 'That was a long time ago, huh' or only swaps one or two low-signal words.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:63; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:137; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:223; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:227; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:261; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:345. The chapter repeatedly uses isolated one-line fragment paragraphs for emphasis: 'Grateful.' 'Ayane.' 'Not the only version.' 'This was different.' 'It was wrong.' 'Seven.'
findings.problem: The fragment-paragraph emphasis becomes a visible prose tic. Once or twice it lands; at this frequency it gives four different POVs the same emphasis mechanism and starts to feel like autopilot rather than scene-specific rhythm.
findings.rewrite_direction: Trim or absorb several of the fragment beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:63, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:137, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:223, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:227, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:261, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:345 into full sentences or adjacent syntax. Keep only the emphases that materially change pressure so Jet, Spike, Faye, and Ed stop sharing the same rhythmic template.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least three of the fragment beats anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:63, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:137, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:223, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:227, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:261, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_12.md:345 are rewritten as full sentences or folded into adjoining sentences, and FAIL if isolated one-line fragments remain the dominant emphasis device across all four scenes.

summary: Fail across all four lenses. The chapter has strong atmosphere and interior access, but it stays too structurally uniform at the chapter level, lets Spike's private register drift into polished moral essay, leaks a calibration-only Ed example line into live dialogue, and overuses clipped fragment emphasis until it becomes a prose tic.
